title: runspec.toml
description: Interface specification for anything runnable — scripts, apps, MCP tools. See https://JasonFinestone.github.io/runspec

autonomy_level.description: How much agent autonomy is permitted. Most restrictive level wins when multiple apply.

config.description: Project-wide defaults. The only reserved top-level key — all other keys are runnable names.

autonomy-default.description: Default autonomy level for runnables that don't declare one.

registry.description: URL of the runspec-registry instance to heartbeat to.

name.description: Instance name reported to the registry. Defaults to the venv directory name.

heartbeat_data.description: Extra data to include in heartbeats. 'system' adds pid and uptime.

runnable.description: A runnable tool — a script, application, or MCP tool described by this file.

description.description: Human and agent readable description of what this tool does.

autonomy-reason.description: Explanation of why this autonomy level was chosen.

output.description: Declares what the runnable writes to stdout. Surfaces as x-output in emitted schemas.

hosts.description: Restrict this runnable to specific hostnames. Absent means available everywhere it is installed.

become_method.description: Privilege escalation method used when run_as is set.

become_flags.description: Optional flags passed to the become method (e.g. "-H" for sudo).

args.description: Argument definitions. Each value is a bare default (type inferred) or a full arg definition.

groups.description: Argument group constraints (exclusive, inclusive, at-least-one, exactly-one, conditional).

commands.description: Subcommands. Each key is a subcommand name with its own args, groups, autonomy, and description.

run_as.description: The OS user to run the command as on the remote host.

oneOf.description: Username or environment variable reference (e.g. "oracle" or "$ORACLE_RUN_AS").

default.description: Fallback user when no host or pattern matches. Empty string means no escalation.

hosts.description: Exact hostname → username mapping. Empty string means no escalation on that host.

patterns.description: Regex pattern → username mapping. Evaluated top-to-bottom, first match wins.

arg_value.description: A bare default value (type inferred) or a full argument definition object.

oneOf.description: Bare boolean — inferred as flag type with this default.
Bare integer — inferred as int type with this default.
Bare float — inferred as float type with this default.
Bare string — inferred as str type with this default.

arg_def.description: Full argument definition (inline table or full [runnable.args.name] block).

type.description: Argument type. Inferred from default or options if omitted.

default.description: Default value. Absence implies required = true (unless required is explicitly set).

required.description: Whether the argument is required. Inferred from missing default if omitted.

description.description: Human and agent readable description. Doubles as a form label.

options.description: Valid choices. Infers type = "choice" when present.

range.description: Valid range for numeric types: [min, max].

multiple.description: Accept multiple values (repeated --flag style). Serialised as newline-delimited in RUNSPEC_* env vars.

delimiter.description: Split a single value by this delimiter (e.g. "," for --fields a,b,c).

env.description: Environment variable fallback. Checked after CLI args, before the default.

deprecated.description: Deprecation message shown when this arg is used.

autonomy.description: Per-arg autonomy override. Escalates to this level if more restrictive than the runnable level.

ui.description: Form control hint. Inferred from type and properties if omitted.

meta.description: Developer-defined metadata. Pass-through — runspec never interprets the contents.

args.description: List of arg names in this group (used by exclusive, inclusive, at-least-one, exactly-one).

exclusive.description: At most one arg from the group may be provided.

inclusive.description: If any arg is provided, all must be provided.

at-least-one.description: One or more args from the group must be provided.

exactly-one.description: Strictly one arg from the group must be provided.

if.description: Trigger arg for a conditional group — when this arg is provided, requires are enforced.

requires.description: Args that become required when the 'if' trigger arg is provided.

autonomy-reason.description: Explanation of why this autonomy level was chosen.

output.description: Declares what this subcommand writes to stdout.

groups.description: Argument group constraints for this subcommand.
